cwe: CWE-924 - Improper Enforcement of Message Integrity During Transmission in a Communication Channel
CWE-836 - Use of Password Hash Instead of Password for Authentication

threat.title: NFC/RFID Relay Attack for Contactless Payment Fraud
threat.description: NFC relay attacks enable attackers to relay communication between a legitimate contactless card/device and a payment terminal or access control reader from distances far beyond the intended NFC range (4-10cm). Attackers use two devices: a 'mole' device placed near the victim's card/phone and a 'proxy' device near the target reader. This attack bypasses proximity-based security and enables unauthorized payments, car theft, and building access without the victim's knowledge.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker positions 'mole' device (hidden NFC reader) near victim's wallet/phone in crowded subway or cafe
2. Attacker holds 'proxy' device (NFC emulator) near payment terminal at store checkout or gas station
3. Relay link established via Bluetooth, WiFi, or 4G/5G between mole and proxy (latency <500ms)
4. Terminal initiates contactless payment request, relayed to victim's card through mole device
5. Victim's card responds with EMV cryptogram, relayed back through proxy to terminal
6. Payment approved without victim removing card from wallet - transaction appears legitimate to issuer
prerequisites: Victim has contactless payment card or mobile payment enabled (NFC active)
No distance bounding or anti-relay mechanisms implemented
EMV transaction velocity checks insufficient or absent
Relay latency under EMV timeout threshold (~500ms for Mastercard, 700ms for Visa)
Victim in physical proximity to attacker's mole device

cve_references.description: iOS Wallet NFC relay vulnerability allowing unauthorized Apple Pay transactions via relay attack
Tesla Model 3 keyless entry relay attack vulnerability enabling vehicle theft
HID iCLASS SE/Seos credential relay attack bypassing secure access control

real_world_breaches.impact: £100 million in contactless fraud losses in 2022; 29% increase from 2021. Relay attacks accounted for estimated 15-20% of cases
real_world_breaches.root_cause: EMV contactless cards lack distance bounding protocols, enabling relay attacks at range

real_world_breaches.incident: Tesla Model S/3/X Relay Theft Wave
real_world_breaches.impact: Over 200 Tesla vehicles stolen in UK using keyless entry relay attacks in Q1 2023. Average vehicle value £60,000
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Tesla's passive keyless entry system vulnerable to relay attack between key fob and vehicle (extended range to 100+ meters)

real_world_breaches.impact: Unauthorized access to 15+ commercial buildings using HID card relay attacks. Security audit found 78% of buildings vulnerable
real_world_breaches.root_cause: MIFARE Classic and HID Prox cards relayed from employee parking areas to lobby readers

real_world_breaches.impact: €2.3 million stolen via NFC relay attacks on ATMs across France, Germany, Netherlands. 1,200+ victims
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Attackers relayed NFC signals from victims' cards to modified ATMs accepting contactless withdrawals

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Apple Pay relay attack vulnerability allowing unauthorized transactions up to contactless limit without device unlock
Google Pay NFC relay bypass enabling payment authentication relay through compromised Android device

security_research.title: Relay Attacks on Passive Keyless Entry and Start Systems in Modern Cars
security_research.key_finding: Demonstrated relay attacks on 10 car manufacturers' keyless entry systems with latency under 50ms, enabling theft from 100+ meters

security_research.title: Practical Relay Attack on Contactless Transactions by Using NFC Mobile Phones
security_research.key_finding: EMV contactless transactions successfully relayed using Android NFC phones with 300-400ms latency, within payment network timeouts

security_research.title: Distance-Bounding Protocols: Verification without Time and Location
security_research.key_finding: Formal verification showing distance bounding protocols prevent relay attacks; only 8% of deployed NFC systems implement them

mitigations.title: Implement Distance Bounding Protocol for NFC Transactions
mitigations.description: Deploy cryptographic distance bounding protocols that measure round-trip time of challenge-response exchanges to detect relay attacks. Transactions are rejected if latency exceeds physical proximity thresholds (typically 1-3ms for legitimate NFC transactions vs 50-500ms for relay attacks).
nist_csf: PR.AC-7 - Users, devices, and other assets are authenticated
DE.CM-1 - Network monitored to detect anomalies
validation_steps: Test with legitimate NFC card at various distances (1cm, 5cm, 10cm)
Attempt relay attack with 50m Bluetooth relay - should fail timing check
Measure false positive rate on production devices (<0.1% acceptable)
Verify integration with existing EMV payment flow
Test in high-interference environments (metal surfaces, multiple cards)

mitigations.title: EMV Transaction Velocity Monitoring and Anomaly Detection
mitigations.description: Implement real-time transaction velocity checks and behavioral analytics to detect suspicious patterns indicative of relay attacks, such as rapid successive transactions at geographically distant locations or unusual transaction timing patterns.

code_example.code:
import json
import time
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Dict, List, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass, asdict
from azure.cosmos import CosmosClient, PartitionKey
from geopy.distance import geodesic
import logging

@dataclass
class Transaction:
    """EMV contactless transaction record"""
    transaction_id: str
    card_pan_hash: str  # Hashed PAN for privacy
    amount: float
    currency: str
    merchant_id: str
    merchant_location: tuple  # (latitude, longitude)
    terminal_id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    transaction_type: str  # 'contactless', 'chip', 'swipe'
    auth_method: str  # 'emv_cryptogram', 'pin', 'signature'
    
    def to_dict(self):
        d = asdict(self)
        d['timestamp'] = self.timestamp.isoformat()
        return d

class EMVRelayAttackDetector:
    """
    Real-time EMV transaction velocity and anomaly detection system
    Detects relay attacks based on:
    - Impossible travel (transactions at distant locations in short time)
    - Abnormal transaction frequency
    - Sequential contactless transactions without chip/PIN fallback
    - Unusual merchant patterns
    """
    
    # Detection thresholds
    MAX_VELOCITY_KMH = 800  # Maximum human travel speed (airplane)
    SUSPICIOUS_VELOCITY_KMH = 200  # Flag for investigation
    MAX_CONTACTLESS_SEQUENCE = 5  # Max consecutive contactless without chip/PIN
    VELOCITY_WINDOW_MINUTES = 60  # Time window for velocity analysis
    FRAUD_SCORE_THRESHOLD = 75  # Score above which transaction is blocked
    
    def __init__(self, cosmos_endpoint: str, cosmos_key: str, database_name: str):
        """Initialize with Azure Cosmos DB connection"""
        self.client = CosmosClient(cosmos_endpoint, cosmos_key)
        self.database = self.client.get_database_client(database_name)
        
        # Transaction history container
        self.transactions_container = self.database.get_container_client('transactions')
        
        # Fraud alerts container
        self.alerts_container = self.database.get_container_client('fraud_alerts')
        
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    
    def analyze_transaction(self, txn: Transaction) -> Dict:
        """
        Analyze transaction for relay attack indicators
        Returns fraud assessment with score and recommended action
        """
        fraud_score = 0
        indicators = []
        
        # Get recent transaction history for this card
        recent_txns = self._get_recent_transactions(
            txn.card_pan_hash,
            window_minutes=self.VELOCITY_WINDOW_MINUTES
        )
        
        # Check 1: Velocity analysis (impossible travel)
        velocity_score, velocity_indicators = self._check_velocity_anomaly(
            txn, recent_txns
        )
        fraud_score += velocity_score
        indicators.extend(velocity_indicators)
        
        # Check 2: Transaction frequency
        frequency_score, freq_indicators = self._check_transaction_frequency(
            txn, recent_txns
        )
        fraud_score += frequency_score
        indicators.extend(freq_indicators)
        
        # Check 3: Contactless transaction sequence
        sequence_score, seq_indicators = self._check_contactless_sequence(
            txn, recent_txns
        )
        fraud_score += sequence_score
        indicators.extend(seq_indicators)
        
        # Check 4: Merchant pattern analysis
        merchant_score, merch_indicators = self._check_merchant_patterns(
            txn, recent_txns
        )
        fraud_score += merchant_score
        indicators.extend(merch_indicators)
        
        # Determine action
        action = self._determine_action(fraud_score)
        
        result = {
            'transaction_id': txn.transaction_id,
            'fraud_score': fraud_score,
            'action': action,
            'indicators': indicators,
            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            'analysis_version': '1.0'
        }
        
        # Store transaction with fraud score
        self._store_transaction(txn, fraud_score)
        
        # Create alert if high risk
        if action in ['BLOCK', 'CHALLENGE']:
            self._create_fraud_alert(result)
        
        return result
    
    def _get_recent_transactions(self, card_pan_hash: str, 
                                 window_minutes: int) -> List[Transaction]:
        """Retrieve recent transactions for card from Cosmos DB"""
        window_start = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(minutes=window_minutes)
        
        query = """
        SELECT * FROM c 
        WHERE c.card_pan_hash = @card_pan_hash 
        AND c.timestamp >= @window_start
        ORDER BY c.timestamp DESC
        """
        
        parameters = [
            {'name': '@card_pan_hash', 'value': card_pan_hash},
            {'name': '@window_start', 'value': window_start.isoformat()}
        ]
        
        results = list(self.transactions_container.query_items(
            query=query,
            parameters=parameters,
            enable_cross_partition_query=True
        ))
        
        # Convert to Transaction objects
        transactions = []
        for item in results:
            txn = Transaction(
                transaction_id=item['transaction_id'],
                card_pan_hash=item['card_pan_hash'],
                amount=item['amount'],
                currency=item['currency'],
                merchant_id=item['merchant_id'],
                merchant_location=tuple(item['merchant_location']),
                terminal_id=item['terminal_id'],
                timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(item['timestamp']),
                transaction_type=item['transaction_type'],
                auth_method=item['auth_method']
            )
            transactions.append(txn)
        
        return transactions
    
    def _check_velocity_anomaly(self, current_txn: Transaction, 
                                recent_txns: List[Transaction]) -> tuple:
        """Check for impossible travel between transaction locations"""
        score = 0
        indicators = []
        
        if not recent_txns:
            return score, indicators
        
        # Compare with most recent transaction
        last_txn = recent_txns[0]
        
        # Calculate distance between locations
        distance_km = geodesic(
            last_txn.merchant_location,
            current_txn.merchant_location
        ).kilometers
        
        # Calculate time difference
        time_diff_hours = (
            current_txn.timestamp - last_txn.timestamp
        ).total_seconds() / 3600
        
        if time_diff_hours > 0:
            velocity_kmh = distance_km / time_diff_hours
            
            # Impossible travel detection
            if velocity_kmh > self.MAX_VELOCITY_KMH:
                score += 50
                indicators.append({
                    'type': 'IMPOSSIBLE_TRAVEL',
                    'severity': 'CRITICAL',
                    'detail': f'Velocity {velocity_kmh:.0f} km/h exceeds maximum {self.MAX_VELOCITY_KMH} km/h',
                    'distance_km': round(distance_km, 2),
                    'time_hours': round(time_diff_hours, 2)
                })
            
            # Suspicious velocity (possible relay attack)
            elif velocity_kmh > self.SUSPICIOUS_VELOCITY_KMH:
                score += 25
                indicators.append({
                    'type': 'SUSPICIOUS_VELOCITY',
                    'severity': 'HIGH',
                    'detail': f'Velocity {velocity_kmh:.0f} km/h exceeds typical {self.SUSPICIOUS_VELOCITY_KMH} km/h',
                    'distance_km': round(distance_km, 2),
                    'time_hours': round(time_diff_hours, 2)
                })
        
        return score, indicators
    
    def _check_transaction_frequency(self, current_txn: Transaction,
                                     recent_txns: List[Transaction]) -> tuple:
        """Detect abnormal transaction frequency patterns"""
        score = 0
        indicators = []
        
        # Count transactions in last 10 minutes
        ten_min_ago = current_txn.timestamp - timedelta(minutes=10)
        recent_count = sum(
            1 for txn in recent_txns 
            if txn.timestamp >= ten_min_ago
        )
        
        if recent_count >= 5:
            score += 30
            indicators.append({
                'type': 'HIGH_FREQUENCY',
                'severity': 'HIGH',
                'detail': f'{recent_count} transactions in 10 minutes (relay attack pattern)',
                'transaction_count': recent_count
            })
        elif recent_count >= 3:
            score += 15
            indicators.append({
                'type': 'ELEVATED_FREQUENCY',
                'severity': 'MEDIUM',
                'detail': f'{recent_count} transactions in 10 minutes',
                'transaction_count': recent_count
            })
        
        return score, indicators
    
    def _check_contactless_sequence(self, current_txn: Transaction,
                                    recent_txns: List[Transaction]) -> tuple:
        """Detect long sequences of contactless without chip/PIN fallback"""
        score = 0
        indicators = []
        
        if current_txn.transaction_type != 'contactless':
            return score, indicators
        
        # Count consecutive contactless transactions
        contactless_count = 1  # Current transaction
        for txn in recent_txns:
            if txn.transaction_type == 'contactless':
                contactless_count += 1
            else:
                break  # Stop at first non-contactless
        
        if contactless_count > self.MAX_CONTACTLESS_SEQUENCE:
            score += 20
            indicators.append({
                'type': 'EXCESSIVE_CONTACTLESS',
                'severity': 'MEDIUM',
                'detail': f'{contactless_count} consecutive contactless transactions (expected chip/PIN verification)',
                'contactless_count': contactless_count
            })
        
        return score, indicators
    
    def _check_merchant_patterns(self, current_txn: Transaction,
                                 recent_txns: List[Transaction]) -> tuple:
        """Analyze merchant patterns for anomalies"""
        score = 0
        indicators = []
        
        if not recent_txns:
            return score, indicators
        
        # Check for rapid merchant switching (relay attack pattern)
        unique_merchants = set(txn.merchant_id for txn in recent_txns[:5])
        unique_merchants.add(current_txn.merchant_id)
        
        if len(unique_merchants) >= 5:
            score += 15
            indicators.append({
                'type': 'MERCHANT_HOPPING',
                'severity': 'MEDIUM',
                'detail': f'{len(unique_merchants)} different merchants in recent transactions',
                'merchant_count': len(unique_merchants)
            })
        
        return score, indicators
    
    def _determine_action(self, fraud_score: int) -> str:
        """Determine action based on fraud score"""
        if fraud_score >= self.FRAUD_SCORE_THRESHOLD:
            return 'BLOCK'
        elif fraud_score >= 50:
            return 'CHALLENGE'  # Request step-up authentication
        elif fraud_score >= 25:
            return 'MONITOR'  # Allow but flag for review
        else:
            return 'ALLOW'
    
    def _store_transaction(self, txn: Transaction, fraud_score: int):
        """Store transaction with fraud assessment in Cosmos DB"""
        doc = txn.to_dict()
        doc['fraud_score'] = fraud_score
        doc['id'] = txn.transaction_id
        
        self.transactions_container.create_item(body=doc)
    
    def _create_fraud_alert(self, analysis_result: Dict):
        """Create fraud alert for investigation"""
        alert = {
            'id': f"alert_{analysis_result['transaction_id']}",
            'transaction_id': analysis_result['transaction_id'],
            'fraud_score': analysis_result['fraud_score'],
            'action': analysis_result['action'],
            'indicators': analysis_result['indicators'],
            'status': 'OPEN',
            'created_at': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            'alert_type': 'RELAY_ATTACK_SUSPECTED'
        }
        
        self.alerts_container.create_item(body=alert)
        
        self.logger.warning(
            f"Fraud alert created: {alert['id']} - Score: {alert['fraud_score']}"
        )

# Azure Function entry point
def main(req) -> dict:
    """
    Azure Function triggered on each contactless transaction
    """
    # Parse transaction from request
    txn_data = req.get_json()
    
    txn = Transaction(
        transaction_id=txn_data['transaction_id'],
        card_pan_hash=hashlib.sha256(txn_data['card_pan'].encode()).hexdigest(),
        amount=txn_data['amount'],
        currency=txn_data['currency'],
        merchant_id=txn_data['merchant_id'],
        merchant_location=tuple(txn_data['merchant_location']),
        terminal_id=txn_data['terminal_id'],
        timestamp=datetime.utcnow(),
        transaction_type=txn_data['transaction_type'],
        auth_method=txn_data['auth_method']
    )
    
    # Analyze transaction
    detector = EMVRelayAttackDetector(
        cosmos_endpoint=os.environ['COSMOS_ENDPOINT'],
        cosmos_key=os.environ['COSMOS_KEY'],
        database_name='fraud_detection'
    )
    
    result = detector.analyze_transaction(txn)
    
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'body': json.dumps(result)
    }

validation_steps: Test with simulated relay attack transactions (2 txns 100km apart in 5 min)
Verify false positive rate < 0.5% on production traffic
Confirm alerts trigger within 2 seconds of suspicious transaction
Test merchant pattern detection with 5 different merchants in 10 min
Validate Cosmos DB partitioning for high throughput (10,000+ TPS)

mitigations.title: Mobile Payment SDK Relay Attack Hardening
mitigations.description: Implement SDK-level protections in mobile payment applications (Apple Pay, Google Pay) including device attestation, secure element binding, and transaction context validation to prevent relay attacks through compromised devices.
nist_csf: PR.AC-7 - Users, devices, and other assets are authenticated
PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms verify software integrity
validation_steps: Test payment with device in Airplane Mode (should fail attestation)
Simulate location change >100m during transaction (should be rejected)
Verify Secure Enclave signature on each transaction
Test transaction timeout after 30 seconds of inactivity
Validate high-value transactions require biometric re-auth

indicators: Multiple transactions at geographically distant locations within minutes
Unusual contactless transaction velocity (>5 transactions in 10 minutes)
Long sequence of contactless transactions without chip/PIN fallback
Transaction timing patterns consistent with relay latency (300-500ms delays)
EMV cryptogram validation failures or unusual transaction responses
Contactless transactions outside normal merchant categories for cardholder
Failed distance bounding protocol checks on NFC-enabled devices

log_queries.query:
// Detect impossible travel in contactless payments
PaymentTransactions
| where TransactionType == "contactless"
| extend PrevTransaction = prev(TransactionId, 1) over (partition by CardHashedPAN order by Timestamp asc)
| extend PrevLocation = prev(MerchantLocation, 1) over (partition by CardHashedPAN order by Timestamp asc)
| extend PrevTimestamp = prev(Timestamp, 1) over (partition by CardHashedPAN order by Timestamp asc)
| extend TimeDiffMinutes = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, PrevTimestamp)
| extend DistanceKm = geo_distance_2points(
    toreal(split(MerchantLocation, ',')[0]), 
    toreal(split(MerchantLocation, ',')[1]),
    toreal(split(PrevLocation, ',')[0]), 
    toreal(split(PrevLocation, ',')[1])
  ) / 1000
| extend VelocityKmh = DistanceKm / (TimeDiffMinutes / 60.0)
| where VelocityKmh > 200  // Suspicious velocity
| project Timestamp, CardHashedPAN, TransactionId, Amount, MerchantId, DistanceKm, TimeDiffMinutes, VelocityKmh
| order by VelocityKmh desc

index=payment_gateway sourcetype=emv_transaction transaction_type=contactless
| sort 0 card_pan_hash _time
| streamstats current=f last(merchant_lat) as prev_lat last(merchant_lon) as prev_lon last(_time) as prev_time by card_pan_hash
| eval time_diff_min = ((_time - prev_time) / 60)
| eval distance_km = 6371 * acos(cos(radians(merchant_lat)) * cos(radians(prev_lat)) * cos(radians(prev_lon) - radians(merchant_lon)) + sin(radians(merchant_lat)) * sin(radians(prev_lat)))
| eval velocity_kmh = distance_km / (time_diff_min / 60)
| where velocity_kmh > 200
| table _time, card_pan_hash, transaction_id, amount, merchant_id, distance_km, time_diff_min, velocity_kmh
| sort - velocity_kmh

fields @timestamp, card_pan_hash, transaction_id, amount, merchant_id, merchant_location
| filter transaction_type = "contactless"
| sort card_pan_hash, @timestamp asc
| stats 
    latest(merchant_location) as current_location,
    earliest(merchant_location) as prev_location,
    latest(@timestamp) as current_time,
    earliest(@timestamp) as prev_time
  by card_pan_hash, bin(5m)
| fields card_pan_hash,
    (current_time - prev_time) / 60000 as time_diff_min,
    current_location,
    prev_location
| filter time_diff_min > 0
# Note: CloudWatch Insights lacks native geo functions - export to Lambda for distance calculation
| limit 1000
